title: The surprisingly small world behind Cambridge and Somerville’s cafe scene
author: Josh Breeden
bullets: Cambridge and Somerville have an interconnected cafe scene linked by shared staff and business collaborations.
Vinal Bakery's Sarah Murphy began selling her English muffins pop-up style, supported by Bagelsaurus owner Mary Ting Hyatt.
New Leaf Espresso in Somerville operates as a cooperative, fostering connections within the broader local cafe network.

title: Person killed in Mattapan shooting, 2 others shot in Dorchester overnight Saturday
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at 28 Woolson St. in Mattapan late Saturday night, police reported.
Two individuals were shot and hospitalized after a separate incident at Geneva Ave. and Everton St. in Dorchester.
Boston police are investigating and have made no arrests in connection with the weekend shootings.

title: Kevin Garnett wasn’t a fan of Jayson Tatum missing Game 7 vs. Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Garnett criticized Jayson Tatum's absence in Game 7 against the Sixers, calling it a morale blow.
Jayson Tatum was ruled out of Game 7 due to left knee stiffness, despite earlier indications he would play.
Paul Pierce defended the decision to sit Tatum, prioritizing his long-term health over immediate playoff needs.

title: 2 people wounded, 1 seriously, after shooting in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Two people were shot on Kingsdale Street in Dorchester; a woman is critically injured and a man has non-life-threatening injuries.
Boston police responded to multiple 911 calls at 69 Kingsdale St. regarding gunshots on Sunday around 6:40 p.m.
No arrests have been made; authorities consider the incident targeted and not a general threat to the public.

title: NBA insider: Atlanta Hawks were interested in trade for Jaylen Brown last year
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Atlanta Hawks showed interest in trading for Jaylen Brown last summer, but talks didn't progress, according to Chris Mannix.
A potential trade was hampered by concerns over the size of Jaylen Brown's contract, then the richest in NBA history.
Despite trade interest and rumors, Jaylen Brown expressed his desire to remain with the Boston Celtics long-term.

title: Woman charged in Southborough fatal crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Abigail Throckmorton, 23, charged with motor vehicle homicide in a fatal Southborough crash on Turnpike Road in February, prosecutors said.
Throckmorton accused of driving on the wrong side at 70 mph, crashing into Nissan, killing Crystal Johnson-Clementi, 37.
Throckmorton, facing multiple charges, was released on recognizance with conditions; scheduled for pretrial hearing on June 30.

title: What’s causing an engine shake in my 10-year-old SUV?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2016 Audi Q5 owner experiences engine shaking, a flashing check engine light, and EPC light indication, suggesting engine misfire.
John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, recommends towing the Audi to prevent further damage caused by continued operation.
The EPC light indicates a potential fault in the throttle system or related electronics, often appearing with a misfire condition.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox fall flat against Rays on Mother’s Day
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle struggled against the Rays, allowing three earned runs on seven hits in five innings.
Trevor Story extended his hitless streak but later doubled and scored; he's batting .200 with 14 strikeouts in six games.
Catcher Mickey Gasper excelled, going 3 for 4 with two doubles; Willson Contreras left the game with a hand contusion.

title: Boston gang members, associates facing slew of firearm, drug trafficking charges
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eight members and associates of Boston gangs indicted for firearm and drug trafficking, including fentanyl and cocaine distribution.
The defendants allegedly sold firearms, including machine guns and conversion devices, across Boston, Brockton, and southeastern Massachusetts.
Federal investigation led to the seizure of 18 firearms and large quantities of fentanyl and crack cocaine, authorities report.

title: Photos: Scenes from a fun, but soggy, Saturday in Greater Boston
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Thousands attended LEGO's Duckling Day at Boston Common, featuring duck-themed activities with LEGO® DUPLO® bricks.
Somerville's PorchFest hosted music performances on porches across the city, attracting large crowds despite the rain.
City Hall Plaza's Mojo Boston Music Festival offered a 10-hour celebration of contemporary music and urban art.
